Slots <10p
—
p / kWh · half-hourly
Half-hourly Agile tariff rates from Octopus Energy in p/kWh. Bars are colour-coded by price band — dark green = negative (you're paid to consume), bright green = under 10p (very cheap), through to red = over 40p (expensive). Switch between Import and Export to compare buy and sell rates. The cheapest overnight slots are where pre-charging the battery pays off most.

Go actual
—
Hypothetical
—
Saving vs Go actual
—
Daily net cost · pence · Go (actual) vs scenario
Daily net energy cost for each tariff: import spend minus export income, in pence. Go actual (green) is what you paid; the amber bars show the hypothetical scenario selected above. On days where the scenario bar is lower, that tariff would have been cheaper. Use the scenario selectors to mix and match import and export tariffs independently.
Cumulative saving vs Go actual · £ · positive = scenario cheaper
Running total of the cost difference between the selected scenario and your actual Go tariff, in pounds. A rising line means the scenario is getting cheaper over time relative to Go; a falling line means Go is winning. This is the headline number: how much you would have saved (or lost) had you been on the alternative tariff since the data begins.
Mode switch detected
—
Pre-switch avg daytime charge
—
Post-switch avg daytime charge
—
Daytime battery charging (after 07:00) · kWh · days with solar yield > 1 kWh
Battery charging that occurs after 07:00 (i.e. from solar, not the overnight grid window) for each day with at least 1 kWh of solar yield. In export-priority mode the inverter sends solar to the grid first — the battery only charges from the overnight off-peak window, so daytime charging is near zero (blue bars). In self-use mode the battery also charges from solar during the day (green bars). The dashed vertical line marks the detected mode switch date.
Decision threshold
—
Depleted days
—
Daily solar yield · red = battery depleted · — min SoC · — 15% threshold
Daily solar yield (bars, left axis) overlaid with the lowest battery SoC reached each day (blue line, right axis), measured from 07:00 after the overnight grid charge. Red bars are days where the battery dropped to ≤15% — all usable charge was consumed before the next off-peak window. When red bars cluster it's a signal to run the inverter in self-use mode so solar can top up the battery mid-day. The amber dashed line marks the 15% depletion threshold; the purple dashed line is the computed yield decision boundary.

kWh · half-hourly
Half-hourly electricity readings from your SMETS2 smart meter in kWh per slot. Import shows energy drawn from the grid; export shows energy sent back. This is the raw metered data used in the cost comparison — it represents your net grid interaction after solar generation and battery activity.
